falsification_conditions.condition: S&P 500 closes above 5,600 on any day in the next 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: The bottom_2024_aug pattern match is broken; that pattern showed lower highs and failed recoveries. A close above 5,600 would indicate a V-shaped recovery inconsistent with the pattern, invalidating the bearish bottom signal and suggesting the 'HOLD' was wrong—should have been bullish.

falsification_conditions.condition: VIX closes below 14.00 for 3 consecutive days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A sustained VIX below 14 would signal complacency and risk-on sentiment, directly contradicting the bottom_2024_aug pattern which typically coincides with elevated volatility (VIX >20). This would mean the market is not capitulating and the 'HOLD' signal missed a bullish continuation.

falsification_conditions.condition: 10Y Treasury yield drops more than 40 bps from current level within 14 days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A rapid rates collapse would indicate flight-to-safety or recession panic, transforming the pattern from a bottom into a crash continuation. The bottom_2024_aug pattern requires stabilization in rates; a 40bp+ drop would mean the macro floor failed and the 'HOLD' should have been 'SELL' or the pattern is misidentified.

doctrine: If you can't list what would prove your call wrong, you don't have a real call.
